ref: Abram was very rich. He owned many cattle, sheep, and goats, and had a lot of silver and gold.

ref: And so, I'll put you under a curse. Because you killed Abel and made his blood run out on the ground, you will never be able to farm it again.

translated: And he spoke the word to him, showed him that, the Hebrew woman who was with him called her name, she said my name is steal your presence from me.

ref: Rachel was very jealous of Leah for having children, and she said to Jacob, “I'll die if you don't give me some children!”
text: Na Rahel hun dɛ ɔnnwo ba mma Jakob no, Rahel n’enyi beer no nua no; na ɔsee Jakob dɛ, Ma me mba, ɔno mubowu.
translated: And Rachel's father was the brother of Jacob, Rachel was the younger daughter; and Jacob saw her and said to him, "I will work seven years for you."

ref: The woman stared at the fruit. It looked beautiful and tasty. She wanted the wisdom that it would give her, and she ate some of the fruit. Her husband was there with her, so she gave some to him, and he ate it too.
text: Na ɔbaa no hun dɛ dua no ye dɛ wodzi, na ɔyɛ enyiwa do fɛw, yɛ akɔndɔ, na otum so dɛ obue enyi no, ɔtseew n’aba no bi dzii; na ɔmaa no kun a ɔka no ho no so bi, na odzii.
translated: The woman saw that the tree was old and she was very afraid; there was a hole, and when the bird entered it, it made a strange sound; and she sent the child to fetch it, but something strange happened, and the child died.

ref: Abraham looked up and saw three men standing nearby. He quickly ran to meet them, bowed with his face to the ground,
text: na ɔmaa n’enyi do, na ɔhwɛe, na, hwɛ, mbarimba baasa gyina ne tsentsendo. Na aber a ohun hɔn no, otutuu mbirika fii n’asesee abowano kehyiaa hɔn kwan, na ɔkotoow famu,
translated: He went to his house and saw, look, many children playing together. And when he saw that there, the frightened ones ran away quickly, and he returned home.

ref: Finally, they dug one more well. There was no quarreling this time, and the well was named “Lots of Room,” because the Lord had given them room and would make them very successful.
text: Na ofii hɔ, na okotuu bura fofor; na ɔnodze wɔammperper ho: na ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Rehobot, na ɔsee dɛ, na afei EWURADZE ama hɛn bea, na yɛbɔwowo wɔ asaase yi do.
translated: And he went out from there, and he dug another well; and he called it Wammperper; and he said, "This is my possession," and he saw that, and afterwards the Lord gave them rest, and they rejoiced in this land.

ref: I asked her who her father was, and she answered, “My father is Bethuel the son of Nahor and Milcah.” At once I put the ring in her nose and the bracelets on her arms.
text: Na mibisaa no dɛ, Woana babaa nye wo? Na ɔsee dɛ, Nahor ba Betuel a Milka woo no maa no no, ne babaa nye me: na medze mpɛtsea no hyɛɛ no hwen, na medze nsakɔnadze no hyehyɛɛ ne nsa.
translated: Then I asked, "Whose child are you?" And he said, "Nahor fathered Betuel, whom Milka bore to him, and his father is me. I have a sister placed under me, and I have a brother to hold his hand."

ref: Because of Sarai, the Lord struck the king and everyone in his palace with terrible diseases.

translated: Then Abram took his wife Sarai and gave her to Pharaoh, and Pharaoh took her from him.

ref: But God came to Abimelech in a dream and said, “You have taken a married woman into your home, and for this you will die!”

translated: Then God sent a plague to Abimelek, and He said to him, "Behold, the woman you took is indeed the wife of a dead man; and her name was Sarah."

ref: his father Jacob said to him, “I want you to go to your brothers. They are with the sheep near Shechem.” “Yes, sir,” Joseph answered.

translated: And Israel saw Joseph there, saying, "Where are your brothers in Shechem? Come, let me bury your father's body." And he saw him, saying, "It is I."

ref: Noah wanted to find out if the water had gone down, so he sent out a dove.

ref: So God created humans to be like himself; he made men and women.

ref: The Lord was standing beside the ladder and said: I am the Lord God who was worshiped by Abraham and Isaac. I will give to you and your family the land on which you are now sleeping.

translated: Now, behold, the LORD has spoken, and He said, "I am the LORD, the God of Abraham and the God of Isaac: the land which He swore to give to our fathers, which He gave to them and their descendants, is their inheritance."

ref: So she laughed and said to herself, “Now that I am worn out and my husband is old, will I really know such happiness?”

translated: And the ancestor of the people, and he crossed over Moab: he is the king of the Moabites, the ancestor of this multitude.

ref: and they had three sons. He named the first one Er;

translated: And the firstborn, the father's son died; and he named him Er.

ref: Jacob had set up camp in the hill country of Gilead, when Laban and his relatives came and set up camp in another part of the hill country. Laban went to Jacob
text: Na Laban too Jakob. Nna Jakob abɔ n’asesee wɔ bepɔw no do: na Laban ɔnye no nkorɔfo so bobɔɔ hɔndze wɔ Gilead bepɔw do.
translated: And Laban went after Jacob. Then Jacob set up a heap of stones there: and Laban took some men and pursued Jacob to Gilead.

ref: Rachel named him Dan, because she said, “God has answered my prayers. He judged in my favor and has given me a son.”
text: Na Rahel see dɛ, Nyankopɔn edzi m’asɛm ama me, na eso oetsie me ndze, na ɔama me babanyin: dɛm ntsi ɔfrɛɛ ne dzin Dan a asekyerɛ nye: ɔsɛndzifo.
translated: Rachel then said, "God has heard my voice and given me; He has also remembered my affliction and given me a son. Now therefore, He who judged me has delivered me."

ref: There is plenty of land for you to choose from. Let's separate. If you go north, I'll go south; if you go south, I'll go north.”
text: Ana nnyɛ asaase no nyinara na ɔda w’enyim yi bi? Meserɛ wo, tsew woho fi mo ho: sɛ bankum na ebɛfa a, ɔno mo so mobɔkɔ nyimfa; anaa sɛ nyimfa na ebɛfa a, ɔno mo so mobɔkɔ bankum.
translated: Are you not the owner of the land who gives your eyes this? I testify to you, you do not understand it: if the fowl is fat, then in its place you put a sheep; or if the sheep is fat, then in its place you put a fowl.

ref: “But sir,” the man replied, “the property is worth only
text: Mo wura, tsie me: dwetɛ dwetɛbona ahaanan asaase nye dɛn wɔ emi na ɔwo ntamu? Dɛm ntsi sie wo fun.
translated: Oh king, listen: Truly, what is wrong with the world, the land, and the people? They are looking at you because of this.

text: na edze akɛma no, ama oedzi, na oeehyira wo ansaana oewu.

translated: And he said, If Esau dies, then he will take the inheritance, and he will take it by force.

ref: Then Abraham lived a long time as a foreigner in the land of the Philistines.
text: Na Abraham yɛɛ hɔho wɔ Filistiafo asaase do nda pii.
translated: And Abraham sojourned in Gerar in the land of the Philistines for many days.

ref: God said, “Now we will make humans, and they will be like us. We will let them rule the fish, the birds, and all other living creatures.”
text: Na Nyankopɔn see dɛ, Wɔmma yɛnyɛ nyimpa wɔ hɛn su do, wɔ hɛn saso do: na wonya tum wɔ po mu nam, nye wimu ntumbowa, nye fiembowa, nye mbowa a wɔwea asaase do nyinara.
translated: As God has said, They have done something new for us, in our mouths and in our ears: and they gave in the wilderness water from the rock, fire from the flame, and bread that rained down on the earth.

ref: I will give you and your family all the land you can see. It will be theirs forever!
text: na asaase a ihu no nyinara, medze bɛma ɔwo, nye w’asefo daa.
translated: And the earth that its face Nyinara gave birth to her child, her child is indeed.

ref: Some years later Judah's wife died, and he mourned for her. He then went with his friend Hirah to the town of Timnah, where his sheep were being sheared.
text: Na ɔwɔ hɔ wɔ hɔ nna Juda ne yer a ɔyɛ Sua ne babaa no, owui. Na Juda no werɛ kyekyeree no, ɔkɔr no nguansusuafo nkyɛn wɔ Timna, ɔno nye ne nyɛnko Adullamnyi Hira.
translated: And he was there when Judah's daughter-in-law Tamar became pregnant by her father-in-law; she died. And Judah did not recognize her, he went to his shepherds in Timna, he was with his friend Hira from Adullam.
